tracks.notes: Available as both a concentration for majors and as a minor for non-majors.
Available as a B.S. degree, B.S. concentration, and as a separate B.S. degree in Artificial Intelligence.
Available as a B.S. degree and a B.S. concentration.
Available as a B.S. concentration and as a minor for non-majors.

Available as a minor for non-majors.

exceptions_and_notes: The Bachelor of Science (B.S.) is ABET accredited.
The Bachelor of Arts (B.A.) offers a more flexible plan for combining with other studies.
There are four minor tracks: algorithm theory, bioinformatics, computer systems, and software development, which are only for non-CS majors.
There are seven concentrations for CS majors, which correspond to specialized areas.

Students interested in the Master’s Accelerated Program (MAP) can apply to take up to 12 graduate credits during their undergraduate studies for dual application to undergraduate and subsequent MS degree; admission requires at least 3.3 GPA and completion of two out of three required 300-level CS courses.
